opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I went outside to get the bins and my fingers turned completely dead white and the pain is excruciating — they feel like they're freezing from the inside out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been 45 minutes and even indoors the pain hasn't gone — I'm scared I'll lose my fingers.

symptom_narrative.onset: after brief cold exposure ~45 minutes ago
symptom_narrative.character: fingers went stark white, then bluish, severe throbbing pain
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: triggered by cold, persisting despite coming indoors
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: both hands equally; fingers starting to go red/blotchy again as they warm; no black areas; no chest pain or breathlessness
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get cold-triggered white fingers (cold-triggered finger colour change) and have a connective tissue condition
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't use hands due to pain

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: cold-triggered finger colour change and a mild connective tissue condition (positive centromere antibodies), mild reflux

access_barriers.transport: neighbour can drive or call an ambulance

objective_data_offered.value: both hands' fingertips went stark white then bluish; as I warm them they're going red/blotchy again; no fingers are black; nails/skin a bit tight
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report over video; cannot check pulses, capillary refill or perform Allen's test remotely
